ingredients.name: Marunouchi Park
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒503-0888 Gifu, Ogaki, Marunouchi, 2 Chome, 地内

ingredients.name: Ohashi Pharmacy Kuruwamachi
ingredients.localName: おおはし薬局 郭町店

ingredients.name: Ogaki Station
ingredients.entityContext: Ogaki's main station is the gateway to the story's world and appears multiple times — including when the group searches for Sahara, Shoko's elementary-school friend, across the city.

ingredients.name: Seifuro
ingredients.address: 190 Yamashitacho, Naka Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa 231-0023, Japan

ingredients.name: OSOZAi+Bar Mino Misho Asty Ogaki
ingredients.localName: OSOZAi+バル美濃味匠 アスティ大垣店

ingredients.name: Yoro Station
ingredients.address: Washinosu, Yoro, Yoro District, Gifu 503-1261, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The small Yoro Line station where Shoya and Shoko arrive for their day trip to Yoro — the starting point of the film's most carefree and visually distinctive sequence.

ingredients.name: CLASSIC BAGEL
ingredients.address: 〒503-0908 Gifu, Ogaki, Nishitogawacho, 2 Chome−26 CLASSIC BAGEL

ingredients.name: Cheesecake Princess
ingredients.localName: チーズケーキプリンセス

ingredients.name: Ogaki Park
ingredients.entityContext: The park where Shoya spends time with Shoko's little sister Yuzuru and niece Maria, and where the group reconvenes during the Ogaki fireworks festival. One of the most-visited A Silent Voice pilgrimage spots in central Ogaki.

ingredients.name: Ogaki Park Fountain
ingredients.entityContext: The fountain plaza within Ogaki Park that recurs in background shots throughout the film — part of the same park setting as the fireworks festival and Yuzuru's scenes.

ingredients.name: Ogaki Machinery Co., Ltd. Ogaki Main Branch
ingredients.localName: 大垣機工（株） 大垣本店

ingredients.name: Police Box at the Gifu Naka Police Station, Station Front
ingredients.localName: 岐阜中警察署 駅前交番

ingredients.name: Gifu Station
ingredients.entityContext: The real-world model for 'Taiyou Girls' Academy Station' in the film — the station where Ishida and Nishimiya unexpectedly run into Sahara, their former elementary-school classmate, in one of the story's most emotionally charged reunions.

ingredients.name: Yoro Park
ingredients.address: 1298-2 Takabayashi, Yoro, Yoro District, Gifu 503-1267, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The expansive nature park in Yoro that frames the film's day-trip sequence, including the walk to Yoro Falls and the visit to the Site of Reversible Destiny.

ingredients.name: Nobunaga Plaza of Dreams
ingredients.entityContext: The open plaza in front of Gifu Station, marked by its golden Oda Nobunaga statue, which forms the backdrop for the Gifu Station arrival and departure scenes in the film — part of the recognizable streetscape the director replicated from Oima's hometown.

ingredients.name: Shiki no Hiroba
ingredients.entityContext: The 'Four Seasons Plaza' in central Ogaki that serves as a background gathering point in the film, part of the recognizable Ogaki downtown streetscape the director closely replicated.

ingredients.name: Former Ibikawa Bridge
ingredients.localName: 国指定重要文化財 旧揖斐川橋梁
ingredients.entityContext: The bridge over the Ibi River near Ogaki, the waterway associated with the film's climactic summer fireworks sequence — featured in the tense, crowded festival scenes that set up the rooftop confrontation.

ingredients.name: Ogakishi Sogofukushi Hall
ingredients.type: コミュニティ センター
categories: コミュニティ センター
ingredients.entityContext: The Ogaki welfare hall where Ishida spots Nishimiya on the upper floor and chases her through the building — the scene where he finally manages to return her notebook to her, one of the film's first genuine moments of reconnection.

ingredients.name: Midori Bridge
ingredients.entityContext: The green bridge over the Suimon River is arguably the emotional center of the film — the site of Shoko's misinterpreted rooftop confession and the bridge Shoya reaches in a desperate race to save her. Every pilgrimage guide to A Silent Voice puts this first.

ingredients.name: Nagashima Spa Land
ingredients.localName: ナガシマスパーランド
ingredients.entityContext: The large amusement park near Kuwana that the friend group visits in a key comic-relief excursion — the rides and crowds appear in one of the film's lighter bonding scenes before the third-act drama.

ingredients.name: Yoro Falls
ingredients.address: 1298-2 Takabayashi, Yoro, Yoro District, Gifu 503-1267, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The famous waterfall deep in Yoro Park that appears in the background of the Yoro excursion scenes — a natural landmark the director used to contrast with the urban Ogaki setting.

ingredients.name: The Site of Reversible Destiny Yoro Park
ingredients.type: アート ギャラリー
categories: アート ギャラリー
ingredients.address: 1298ｰ2 Takabayashi, Yoro, Yoro District, Gifu 503-1267, Japan
ingredients.entityContext: The surreal outdoor art installation in Yoro where Shoya takes Shoko on their date — the bumpy, disorienting terrain of Arakawa and Gins's Reversible Destiny is recreated almost shot-for-shot in the film's lightest and most hopeful sequence.

ingredients.name: Bairin Tei
ingredients.address: Japan, 〒503-1254 Gifu, Yoro District, Yoro, Yorokoen, 1274-1
